original_hypothesis.title: Missing Fee Upper Bound Validation Allows Owner to DoS All Claims via Underflow in _deductFees
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: ILOManager.setPlatformFee and setPerformanceFee accept any uint16 value (up to 65535) with no upper bound check. If PLATFORM_FEE is set >= 10000 BPS (100%), _deductFees computes: amount0Left = amount0 - FullMath.mulDiv(amount0, feeBPS, 10000). When feeBPS >= 10000, the subtracted value >= amount0, causing underflow in Solidity 0.7.6 (no overflow protection). This produces a massive erroneous amount that fails on transfer, permanently DOSing all claim() calls. Fees are snapshot at project creation time in _cachedProject, so this affects all pools created after the fee change.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: uint16 can hold values up to 65535 but BPS should be < 10000. No validation exists, and the fee is applied in unchecked arithmetic.

proof_of_signal.pattern: setPlatformFee(uint16 _platformFee) has no require(_platformFee < BPS); _deductFees subtracts fee amount from total with no overflow check in Solidity 0.7.6

preconditions: ILOManager owner sets PLATFORM_FEE or PERFORMANCE_FEE >= 10000
New project is created after fee change (fees snapshot at creation)
Pool launches and investors attempt to claim

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: No bounds check on fee setter; Solidity 0.7.6 unchecked arithmetic in _deductFees
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Requires ILOManager owner to set excessive fees
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: After fee change, for all new projects

operational_questions: Can fees be changed retroactively for existing projects?
Does the uint16 type limit provide any practical protection?
What is the exact revert behavior in _deductFees with fee >= 10000?
validation_requirements: Set PLATFORM_FEE to 10001, create project, launch pool, attempt claim

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: New project created with fee=10001 snapshot
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: All claim() calls revert due to underflow in _deductFees

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: All investor claims permanently blocked for affected projects
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: No recovery mechanism - funds locked in Uniswap position
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: claim() for all pools created after fee change

investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Fee changes followed by new project creation

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
